info.title: elvia-edna-events
info.description: A tool for working with Elvia Events from the command line

commands.name: avro-create
commands.description: Create an Avro schema for a .NET type.

options.name: --assembly
options.description: The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple space-separated values accepted).

options.name: --enum-behavior
options.description: The type of schema that enum types should be represented by. Options are "symbolic" (generate an "enum" schema; the default behavior), "integral" (generate an "int" or "long" schema based on the underlying type; the behavior for all flag enums), and "nominal" (generate a "string" schema).

options.name: --nullable-references
options.description: Which reference types should be represented with nullable union schemas. Options are "annotated" (use nullable annotations if available; the default behavior), "none", and "all". [default: 2]

options.name: --temporal-behavior
options.description: Whether timestamps should be represented with "string" schemas (ISO 8601) or "long" schemas (timestamp logical types). Options are "iso8601", "epochmilliseconds", and "epochmicroseconds".

options.name: --type
options.description: The type to build a schema for.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

commands.name: code-generate
commands.description: Generate C# code for a schema from the Schema Registry or stdin.

options.name: --registry-config
options.description: Configuration options to provide to the registry client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs accepted).

options.name: --registry-url
options.description: The URL of the schema registry.

options.name: --id
options.description: If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID of the schema.

options.name: --subject
options.description: If an ID is not specified, the subject of the schema.

options.name: --version
options.description: The version of the schema.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The value subject topic name

options.name: --system
options.description: The system/namespace for the consuming application. Used for fetching schema registry credentials from Vault.

options.name: --nullable-references
options.description: Whether reference types selected for nullable record fields should be annotated as nullable. [default: True]

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.description: If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID of the schema.

options.name: --subject

options.name: --version
options.description: The version of the schema.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The value subject topic name

options.name: --system
options.description: The system/namespace for the consuming application. Used for fetching schema registry credentials from Vault.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --registry-url
options.description: The URL of the schema registry.

options.name: --id
options.description: If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID of the schema.

options.name: --subject

commands.description: Retrieve versions of schema from the Schema Registry.

commands.name: registry-delete
commands.description: Delete schema for a given topic.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The topic name to delete all schema versions for.

options.name: --permanent
options.description: Should the schema be deleted permanently? [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

commands.name: kafka-topic-delete
commands.description: Delete a given topic. Must be recreated manually in Terraform.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The topic name to delete.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version

commands.name: kafka-topic-messages-delete
commands.description: Delete messages in a given topic. Only if NOT using compacting.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The topic to delete messages in.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --system
options.description: What system are you authenticating through?

options.name: --list
options.description: Which type of metadata should be listed (topics or groups)? [default: topics]

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --group
options.description: Consumer group. Your user must have access to the consumer groups system.

options.name: --offset
options.description: Comma separated list of partition offsets. Either position based '124,567' or key/value with partition=offset '0=124,1=5678'
